misconceptionSummary: Students identify the first few terms correctly but continue with the wrong sequence rule.

steps.narration: After sixteen comes twenty-five, not twenty.

lines.text: The gaps are +3, +5, +7, so the next gap is +9

steps.narration: That is another way to see why twenty-five must come next.

visuals.expression: Check the defining rule before continuing
visuals.label: Recognise the sequence type accurately

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, confirm which special sequence you are working with before extending it.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: spotting a familiar start but then continuing by guesswork. Use the defining rule of the special sequence.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Once you know the type of sequence, let its rule control the next term.
